hypothesis: If training splits reduce template-frequency and structural-similarity bias via broad, diverse sampling, then single-step retrosynthesis models—especially template-free architectures—will achieve higher top-k and round-trip accuracy on Pistachio OOD (unseen templates) with smaller generalization gaps than models trained on narrow, frequency-biased splits because mitigating dataset bias curtails template memorization and promotes learning of generalizable chemical disconnections.
